roles.title: HRIS APPLICATION AND PRODUCT CONSULTANT
responsibilities: In my role as HRIS Application and Product Consultant at Microtrek Develop from September 1995 to March 1996, I specialized in bespoke HR and payroll application development.
This position demanded a deep understanding of both client business processes and technical solutions, allowing me to bridge the gap between functional requirements and software capabilities.

Key Responsibilities and Achievements: Product Development and Customization: Led the design and development of customized HR and payroll applications tailored to meet specific client needs, enhancing their operational efficiency and compliance with regulatory standards.

Client Consultation: Conducted thorough needs assessments with clients to understand their specific HRIS requirements, which guided the customization of software solutions to better align with their business operations.
Training and User Education: Developed comprehensive training programs and materials for clients, ensuring they were fully equipped to utilize the new systems effectively.
This involved both on-site training sessions and the creation of user manuals.
Project Management: Managed the full project lifecycle from initial client consultation through to implementation and post-go-live support, ensuring projects were delivered on time and within budget.

Technical Support and Maintenance: Provided ongoing support and maintenance for HRIS applications, addressing any issues promptly and efficiently to minimize downtime and maintain client satisfaction.
achievements: In my role as HRIS Application and Product Consultant at Microtrek Develop from September 1995 to March 1996, I specialized in bespoke HR and payroll application development.

roles.title: HRIS and SYSTEMS CONSULTANT
responsibilities: During my tenure at Best Software from March 1996 to October 1998, as an HRIS and Systems Consultant, I played a pivotal role in launching and establishing the HR/Pay product suite in the Canadian market.

My responsibilities encompassed a broad range of activities from client consulting to systems implementation, focusing on optimizing human resources information systems for enhanced functionality and user engagement.
Key Responsibilities and Achievements:

Market Launch and Strategy: Led the successful launch of the Abra HR/Pay product in Canada, crafting and executing a comprehensive market entry strategy that included sales development, quality assurance, and client engagements.
Client System Customization: Delivered customized HRIS solutions that catered to the specific needs of various industries, enhancing client operations and data management capabilities.
Training and Development: Developed and implemented extensive training programs for end-users and IT staff, ensuring seamless adoption and proficient use of the HRIS applications.

Product Testing and Quality Assurance: Oversaw product testing phases to ensure software functionality met stringent quality standards before rollouts, significantly reducing post-launch issues and enhancing customer satisfaction.
Documentation and Support: Created detailed documentation for HRIS applications, facilitating easier understanding and use by clients.
achievements: During my tenure at Best Software from March 1996 to October 1998, as an HRIS and Systems Consultant, I played a pivotal role in launching and establishing the HR/Pay product suite in the Canadian market.

Sales and Marketing Support: Collaborated closely with the sales and marketing teams to promote HRIS products, contributing to a 40% increase in client acquisition within the first two years of product launch.

roles.title: ERP, HRIS AND SYSTEMS CONSULTANT
responsibilities: At Second Foundation Inc., from October 1998 to November 2000, I held the role of ERP, HRMS, HRIS Systems Consultant, where I specialized in implementing and optimizing Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and Human Resources Management Systems (HRMS) across a variety of client industries.
This role involved deep technical expertise, client engagement, and project management to enhance operational efficiencies and align IT systems with business strategies.
Key Responsibilities and Achievements:
Customized ERP Solutions: Developed and implemented tailored ERP solutions for clients, focusing on enhancing functional capabilities and integration with existing client systems.
HRMS Expertise: Spearheaded the deployment of comprehensive HRMS solutions, improving clients' human resources operations through advanced data management and process automation.

Project Leadership: Managed multiple large-scale projects from inception to completion, ensuring adherence to timelines, budget, and scope while delivering high-quality solutions that met specific client needs.
Client Consultation and Strategy: Provided strategic advice to clients on system selection and optimization, helping them choose the right tools and technologies to support their business objectives.
Training and Support: Designed and conducted training programs for client teams, ensuring smooth transition and adoption of new systems.
achievements: At Second Foundation Inc., from October 1998 to November 2000, I held the role of ERP, HRMS, HRIS Systems Consultant, where I specialized in implementing and optimizing Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and Human Resources Management Systems (HRMS) across a variety of client industries.

roles.title: MANAGER OF BUSINESS APPLICATIONS, TRAINING AND SUPPORT
responsibilities: In my role as Manager of Business Applications, Training, and Support at Kuntz Electroplating, I was instrumental in overseeing a significant evolution of our business application services.
This encompassed a strategic overhaul of our human capital management systems, driving technological modernization, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.

The role included the following key contributions and responsibilities: -> Cost-Efficient Support Operations: I led the repatriation of ERP and HRMS support services, strategically reducing support costs by more than $1 million over three years, achieving a 90% reduction in expenses. -> Project Audit and Methodology Implementation: Conducted rigorous audits of ongoing projects to pinpoint improvement opportunities and implemented a formal project methodology based on the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMI-PMBOK), elevating our project management standards. -> Tier 1 HRMS Implementation Leadership: Managed a cross-functional team through the comprehensive analysis, selection, and implementation of a Tier 1 Human Resource Management System, streamlining operations and enhancing system integration. -> Lawson HRMS System Overhaul: Oversaw the successful implementation of the Lawson HRMS system, which replaced legacy systems and numerous custom bolt-ons.

This initiative resulted in a fourfold improvement in payroll processing timelines and the creation of a singular, organization-wide HR payroll data repository, marking it as the first project completed on schedule and within budget. -> Self-Service Portal Advancements: Managed and implemented the Lawson Employee and Manager Self-Service 8.1, which significantly improved accessibility and autonomy for staff, contributing to higher productivity and engagement. -> Adoption of New Management Methodologies: Took an active part in the adoption of new project management and systems development methodologies, ensuring that our practices were cutting-edge and aligned with industry best practices.

My tenure at Kuntz Electroplating was defined by a commitment to operational excellence, leveraging technology to not only meet but anticipate business needs, while always ensuring that initiatives were delivered on time and within budget.

roles.title: ERP PROJECT MANAGER AND ERP CONSULTANT
responsibilities: During my tenure at Hubble Technology Consulting Inc., from 2005 to 2007, I held the role of ERP Project Manager and Consultant, specializing in Lawson ERP systems.

This position required a blend of technical acumen, strategic planning, and client engagement to successfully deliver customized ERP solutions that met specific client needs while optimizing their operational processes.
Key Responsibilities and Achievements:
ERP System Implementation and Upgrades: Led several high-profile ERP implementation and upgrade projects, which included comprehensive planning, configuration, testing, and deployment phases.
My efforts ensured that ERP systems were tailored to enhance client operations, with a focus on achieving 'zero downtime' during live system upgrades.
Client Relationship Management: Developed and maintained strong relationships with clients across various industries, providing strategic ERP solutions and continuous support.
I successfully managed client expectations and collaborated closely with stakeholders to ensure solutions aligned with their business goals.
Custom Solution Development: Designed and implemented custom functionalities within ERP systems to address unique business requirements, enhancing process efficiency and data accuracy for clients.
achievements: During my tenure at Hubble Technology Consulting Inc., from 2005 to 2007, I held the role of ERP Project Manager and Consultant, specializing in Lawson ERP systems.

roles.title: TEAM LEADER - SERVICE DELIVERY AND SUPPORT
responsibilities: As the Team Leader of Service Delivery and Support for the Web at Manulife Financial, my role was to helm a high-performing team, driving excellence in IT service and support that not only upheld but enhanced our service standards.
This position involved strategic oversight of key support operations and the implementation of initiatives that markedly elevated our customer service metrics.

The role included the following key contributions and responsibilities: -> Internet Release Management: Managed all facets of Internet releases, orchestrating cross-functional team efforts, steering Change Management processes, Scope Management, and ensuring clear and concise Stakeholder Communications. -> E-commerce Support Team Leadership: Directed the day-to-day operations of a dynamic e-commerce support team composed of Developers, Business Analysts, Contractors, and Vendors, delivering value-added solutions to both internal and external customers. -> Vendor and Partnership Oversight: Oversaw the management of delivery and third-party partnerships, ensuring collaborative success and service excellence in all vendor relations and interactions. -> Business Relationship Management: Managed a multi-tiered network of business relationships, engaging with stakeholder control groups, marketing partners, steering committees, and business leaders to foster a unified approach to IT service delivery. -> Team Development and Performance Optimization: Cultivated a high-performance team environment through individual coaching and mentoring, enhancing team skills and driving professional development. -> Operational Efficiency Initiatives: Identified and championed support incentives aimed at improving operational efficiencies, ensuring that our support operations were not only effective but also continuously evolving. -> Service Management Methodology: Developed and implemented a unified Service Management methodology, aimed at increasing productivity and elevating the quality of IT services provided. -> Strategic Offshoring and Partnering: Actively supported strategic initiatives by leveraging offshoring and partnering programs to enhance service delivery and capitalize on global efficiencies. -> In this role, I was committed to advancing service delivery standards, fostering a culture of continuous improvement, and leading my team to deliver IT solutions that not only met but also anticipated the evolving needs of our customers.

roles.title: PROJECT MANAGER - INFORMATION SYSTEMS
responsibilities: In my role at Manulife Financial from 2007 to 2011, I served as both Project Manager for Information Systems and Team Leader for Service Delivery and Support.

This dual capacity allowed me to oversee a broad range of IT initiatives, particularly focusing on optimizing information systems management and enhancing service delivery to support Manulife’s strategic business objectives.
Key Responsibilities and Achievements:
Comprehensive Project Management: Led and delivered a wide array of projects within the information systems department, ensuring they met all operational, budgetary, and scheduling targets.
My strategic planning and effective management ensured projects aligned with Manulife's IT governance standards and business strategy.
Service Delivery Excellence: Directed a team responsible for the seamless delivery of IT services, enhancing system reliability and user satisfaction across the organization.
I implemented key performance indicators and service level agreements that improved service delivery metrics and client satisfaction rates.
Vendor and Stakeholder Management: Managed relationships with key vendors and stakeholders, ensuring contractual commitments were met and aligned with Manulife’s strategic goals.
achievements: In my role at Manulife Financial from 2007 to 2011, I served as both Project Manager for Information Systems and Team Leader for Service Delivery and Support.

roles.title: SR. PROJECT MANAGEMENT – IT GROUP BENEFITS
responsibilities: In my senior project management role within IT Group Benefits at Sun Life Financial, I led significant initiatives such as the Health and Dental Paperless Program, which were designed to streamline processes and enhance the overall client experience.

This role required a deft hand in managing complex, multi-year initiatives, ensuring the delivery of solutions that were not only technologically advanced but also user-friendly and environmentally conscious.

The role included the following key contributions and responsibilities: -> Innovative Program Management: Managed a multi-year, multi-million dollar Health and Dental Claims Paperless Program, transforming the client experience by providing a faster, more efficient, and eco-friendly system for plan sponsors and members. -> Strategic Project Planning: Developed detailed project plans that effectively balanced scope, time, cost, quality, resources, risk, communication, and procurement, enabling highly coordinated project execution and oversight. -> Team Leadership: Spearheaded a diverse project team, including onshore and offshore members, fostering a culture of motivation, skill development, and high performance.

Provided coaching and advice to ensure team members were equipped to meet project goals. -> Disaster Recovery Coordination: Took on the responsibility of Milestone Coordinator for a Global Disaster Recovery exercise, emphasizing the criticality of business continuity and preparedness across the organization. -> CMMI-DEV Adoption and Expertise: Actively contributed to the adoption of CMMI-DEV processes, serving as a subject matter expert and internal auditor to drive higher standards of software development and project delivery within the team. -> This role underscored my capacity to lead complex IT projects with a keen eye on delivering value to the business, fostering innovation, and driving operational excellence.
It demonstrated my commitment to leveraging technology to meet the evolving needs of clients and the business alike.

roles.title: SR. PROJECT MANAGER – IT INDIVIDUAL APPLICATIONS
responsibilities: As a Senior Project Manager for IT Individual Applications at Sun Life Financial, my role encompassed leading strategic IT projects that were aligned with individual user needs and overarching business objectives.
The projects I managed were pivotal, both in terms of scale and impact, and spanned various business units and teams, including key initiatives like Anti-Money Laundering and Tax Year-End operations.

The role included the following key contributions and responsibilities: -> Project Leadership and Execution: Spearheaded the planning, execution, and delivery of major IT application projects, including the high-stakes Anti Money Laundering, Tax Year End, and Health and Service Operations Portfolio, ensuring these projects met the dual mandate of regulatory compliance and operational excellence. -> Budget and Resource Management: Managed multimillion-dollar budgets across multiple business units, ensuring resources were allocated effectively and projects delivered maximum value to the organization. -> Stakeholder Engagement: Oversaw the delivery relationship of the Individual Services Operation Portfolio, actively engaging with business partners and ensuring stakeholder expectations were met or exceeded. -> Methodology Development and Application: Developed and applied a tailored Project Framework that was in sync with SDLC methodologies, facilitating streamlined project execution and delivery. -> Team Leadership: Managed and coached offshore Project Management Assistants, driving team performance and fostering a culture of continuous improvement and professional development. -> Quality and Compliance: Actively engaged in CMMI-DEV adoption, leading critical legislative projects that not only complied with stringent regulations but also effectively saved the company from significant potential fines. -> Strategic Project Management: For Group Benefits, led a multi-year Health and Dental Claims Paperless Program, aiming to enhance customer experience while also aligning with environmental sustainability goals. -> Project Coordination and Control: Developed comprehensive project plans that effectively balanced scope, time, cost, quality, resources, and risk, enabling highly coordinated project execution and control. -> Global Initiative Coordination: Served as the Milestone Coordinator for a Global Disaster Recovery exercise, underscoring the critical nature of business continuity planning and execution. -> Subject Matter Expertise: Provided expertise as a CMMI-DEV subject matter expert and internal auditor, contributing to the organization's pursuit of excellence in development practices. -> This role required a robust blend of project management acumen, technical knowledge, and leadership skills, all aimed at delivering IT solutions that supported Sun Life Financial's commitment to providing exceptional client service and maintaining its position as a leader in the financial services industry.

roles.title: MANAGER OF APPLICATIONS, DEVELOPMENT AND SUPPORT
responsibilities: In my role as Manager of Applications, Development, and Support at Spaenaur Inc., I led a team of technology professionals who were responsible for providing specialized expertise across all enterprise application systems.

My comprehensive management of the business applications portfolio encompassed both strategic leadership and hands-on development to drive system functionality, user experience, and operational efficiency to new heights.

The role included the following key contributions and responsibilities: -> Leadership and Team Management: Led and managed a dedicated team of technology professionals, orchestrating specialized support and ensuring that enterprise application systems were not only stable but also equipped to meet the evolving demands of the business. -> Systems Stability and Business Alignment: Vigilantly ensured the robustness and readiness of applications to support business functions, playing a pivotal role in aligning technology solutions with business strategies. -> Technology Research and Implementation: Proactively researched and implemented cutting-edge technology solutions that positioned Spaenaur at the forefront of operational efficiency and technological innovation. -> Project Management and Executive Communication: Managed resource allocation and task assignments for project implementations, ensuring seamless communication with the Executive Management Team and adherence to project timelines. -> Operational Best Practices Development: Developed and instituted best operational practices, focusing on areas such as change management, requirements gathering, and support practices, to promote a culture of excellence and continuous improvement. -> Application Authority Framework: Established and continuously refined a framework for application authority requests, standardizing the use of all enterprise applications to ensure governance and control. -> Enterprise Solutions Assessment: Conducted thorough research and assessments of enterprise-level application technologies, recommending solutions that met current and anticipated business needs. -> Workplace Culture and Climate: Maintained a workplace culture that was recognized as a choice environment, fostering a positive climate that encouraged professional growth, collaboration, and innovation. -> This role required a strategic vision for application lifecycle management and a commitment to continuous improvement, ensuring that the technology underpinning the organization's operations was not only effective but also agile and responsive to the rapid pace of business change.

roles.title: DIRECTOR (ACTING) OF INFORMATION SERVICES
responsibilities: During my tenure at Spaenaur Inc., I served in dual capacities, initially as the Manager of Applications, Development, and Support, and subsequently stepping into the role of Acting Director of Information Services.

My responsibilities encompassed overseeing the IT strategy and operations, ensuring robust application development and support, and driving significant IT projects that aligned with strategic business objectives.
Key Responsibilities and Achievements:

Strategic IT Leadership: As Acting Director, I was responsible for setting the strategic direction of the IT department, aligning technology initiatives with business goals, and overseeing the implementation of critical IT projects.
I successfully managed the IT budget, ensuring investments were made in technologies that drove business value.
Application Management and Development: Oversaw the design, development, and management of enterprise application systems, ensuring they supported business processes and user needs effectively.
I led initiatives to enhance software development frameworks and application performance metrics.

Project Management and Delivery: Directed multiple high-stakes IT projects, including ERP system upgrades and custom software developments, ensuring they were delivered on time, within budget, and met predefined business objectives.

roles.title: IT RISK AND COMPLIANCE LEADER
responsibilities: During my tenure at Economical Insurance from 2015 to 2017, I held the pivotal role of IT Risk and Compliance Lead.
My responsibilities and contributions included:
Strategic IT Risk Management: Developed and executed a revamped IT risk management strategy that aligned with OSFI, Bill 198, ICoFR, and ITGC requirements.
My strategic oversight significantly enhanced operational effectiveness, ensuring compliance with both internal policies and external regulatory obligations.
Compliance Framework Implementation: Spearheaded the creation and operationalization of a comprehensive governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) framework.

This initiative included integrating advanced GRC tools like RSA Archer and ServiceNow, which automated risk assessments and compliance monitoring, thereby reducing manual overhead and enhancing accuracy.
Policy Development and Standardization: Authored and continuously updated IT security policies and standards to reflect evolving industry regulations and organizational objectives.
My efforts ensured that the company's IT practices not only met but often exceeded regulatory requirements, safeguarding against potential compliance issues.
achievements: During my tenure at Economical Insurance from 2015 to 2017, I held the pivotal role of IT Risk and Compliance Lead.

roles.title: IT RISK MANAGEMENT CONSULTANT, GROUP BENEFITS AND RETIREMENT SOLUTION TECHNOLOGIES
responsibilities: In my role as IT Risk Management Consultant for Group Benefits and Retirement Solution Technologies at Manulife Financial, I was integral in fortifying the company's risk posture for our financial and retirement planning technology platforms.

My specialization in risk management extended to both strategic advisory and hands-on operational improvements, ensuring the protection of systems and data integrity critical for seamless operations of our financial products.

The role included the following key contributions and responsibilities: Team Development and Leadership: I established and enhanced a back-office offshore team, focusing on IT Audit, Controls, and Compliance assessments, ensuring they were well-equipped and resilient against potential risks and vulnerabilities. -> Agile Best Practices Coaching: Mentored team members and partners on IT Audit, Controls, and Compliance, emphasizing the adoption of operational best practices within an Agile Delivery environment.

This guidance was pivotal in navigating the transition to new working methodologies and maintaining operational excellence. -> Audit Process Management: Drove and managed comprehensive audit processes, working collaboratively with both internal and external audit teams to facilitate the execution and delivery of audit activities.

This included monitoring the audit lifecycle, from initiation to the timely completion of follow-ups on audit findings. -> Audit Reporting and Response: Handled audit management reporting, coordinated management responses, and oversaw the progress of audit response plans, ensuring all actions were tracked, transparent, and addressed within designated timeframes. -> Consulting and Support for Controls and Compliance: Provided essential consulting support across various programs, contributing expertise on Controls, Audit, and Compliance to reinforce program team initiatives and risk mitigation strategies. -> Third-Party Security Assessments: Supported comprehensive security assessments of third-party vendors, including conducting self-assessment questionnaires, site visits, and analysis of external audit reports to ensure alignment with our stringent security standards. -> Information Security and Compliance Advisory: Offered expert advice to business units on information security, compliance, and control requirements, translating complex regulations into actionable and compliant operational practices. -> Offshore Team Coaching: Coached and mentored offshore teams to enhance their performance and alignment with our Client Management Program's objectives, fostering a culture of continuous learning and professional growth. -> Program Delivery and Service Catalogue Development: Matured existing practices into more formalized Program Delivery models, contributing to the development of a comprehensive Service Catalogue that detailed the range of services offered and operational benchmarks. -> Client Management Program Oversight: Oversaw and actively supported the Client Management Audit, Controls, and Security Assessments Program, ensuring a robust defense against risks and a commitment to maintaining Manulife's reputation for secure and trustworthy client management. -> Incorporating these experiences into my career narrative for the application to Cayenta’s ERP Application Consultant posting, I emphasize the depth of my expertise in risk management and its direct applicability to the role, underscoring my ability to lead, innovate, and secure complex IT ecosystems within large financial institutions.

responsibilities: In my tenure at Manulife from 2017 to 2019, I served as the IS Risk Management Consultant within the Technology Risk Management and Global Information Risk Management divisions.
My responsibilities and achievements included:
Framework Development and Strategic Oversight: Designed and executed the technology risk management framework, focusing on integrating advanced risk assessment tools like RSA Archer.
This initiative enhanced the alignment of risk management strategies with business operations across global departments, significantly mitigating potential risks and improving compliance.

Third-Party Risk Management: Managed a comprehensive third-party management program that included conducting detailed risk assessments and audits (including cSOX and CSAE3416) of all significant vendors.
This ensured that third-party engagements were compliant with stringent regulatory and company standards and supported strategic business objectives.
Innovative Solutions for Risk Challenges: Leveraged integrated risk management tools to embed best practices and control mechanisms across the organization.
This included customizing and deploying GRC platforms such as Onspring and OneTrust to meet specific operational needs, thereby enhancing the company’s overall security and compliance posture.

roles.title: Guest Speaker - Post Graduate
responsibilities: Since 2019, I have served as a Professional Development Speaker and Presenter, engaging with industry professionals and academic audiences at conferences, seminars, and colleges.
This role leverages my extensive expertise in IT and information security to educate and inspire others in the field of project management, risk mitigation, and best practices in IT governance.
Key Responsibilities and Achievements:

Educational Program Design: Developed and delivered high-impact educational content tailored to the needs of professionals and students in the fields of IT and information security, emphasizing the application of industry best practices and innovations.

Public Speaking and Presentations: Delivered presentations at various industry conferences and educational institutions, effectively communicating complex IT concepts and strategies to diverse audiences.
Engagement with Academic Institutions: Collaborated with colleges such as Fanshawe College to provide guest lectures and workshops, enhancing the curriculum with real-world insights and expertise.

Leadership in Professional Development: Guided IT professionals through continuous learning paths, highlighting the importance of certifications and up-to-date knowledge in maintaining competitive advantages in the field.
Stakeholder Engagement: Worked closely with educational leaders and industry experts to align presentation content with current trends and workforce needs, ensuring relevance and applicability.
achievements: Since 2019, I have served as a Professional Development Speaker and Presenter, engaging with industry professionals and academic audiences at conferences, seminars, and colleges.

roles.title: MANAGER INFORMATION SECURITY, GLOBAL INFORMATION SECURITY, GOVERNANCE & RISK
responsibilities: As the Manager of Information Security at OpenText from 2020 to 2023, I directed the Global Information Security, Governance, and Risk Management Program, focusing on the development, oversight, and enhancement of the company's security posture across international operations.
My role involved:

Strategic Leadership and Program Management: Spearheaded the design and execution of the global security risk management strategy, aligning security initiatives with enterprise-wide business goals to minimize risks and ensure compliance with international data protection regulations like GDPR and SOX.
Risk Assessment and Compliance Oversight: Led over 550 third-party risk assessments annually, significantly enhancing the organization’s risk framework and compliance with regulatory requirements.
Managed the complete lifecycle of information security from risk identification to mitigation, ensuring continuous adaptation to emerging threats and changes in the compliance landscape.

GRC Platform Integration and Optimization: Championed the adoption and customization of GRC tools such as Onspring and OneTrust, which involved tailoring these platforms to meet specific departmental needs while enhancing overall performance and compliance.
Team Leadership and Development: Built and managed a high-performance team of both offshore and onshore security professionals.
Provided mentorship and development, fostering a culture of security awareness and proactive risk management.
achievements: As the Manager of Information Security at OpenText from 2020 to 2023, I directed the Global Information Security, Governance, and Risk Management Program, focusing on the development, oversight, and enhancement of the company's security posture across international operations.
Risk Assessment and Compliance Oversight: Led over 550 third-party risk assessments annually, significantly enhancing the organization’s risk framework and compliance with regulatory requirements.
